text: The patient is located in 30 degree right lateral decubitus position and a 2 cm incision is made at the 7th intercostal space on the posterior axillary line.
As it is observed, the initial inspection reveals the left lung collapsed, the heart and left diaphragm.
So the procedure starts by the careful dissection of the cardiophrenic fat tissue including cardiophrenic lymph nodes.
The specimen is finally removed and sent for final histology diagnosis.
That's all and we hope this video has been useful for you.
